_README: Dave: this is the Studio-tool safety map. Run /checkup to list the REAL tool names your built-in Roblox Studio MCP exposes, then classify each. TIERS: 'auto' = safe reads / console / mode / play start-stop (no prompt). An ARTIFACT tool that installs a script -> use the object form {"tier":"artifact","field":"<name of the tool's code argument>"}; the gate checks ONLY that field against a checker-approved file and blocks anything else riding along. A model-INSERT tool that can target a parent -> {"tier":"quarantine","field":"<name of the parent argument>"}; the gate requires that field to equal ServerStorage/ToolboxQuarantine. 'dave' = arbitrary code / model insert / anything you're unsure of (blocked, comes to you). ANY tool NOT listed here is treated as 'dave'. Leave run_code on 'dave' until the /checkup gate canary passes, then switch it to {"tier":"artifact","field":"<its code arg name>"}. IMPORTANT: only mark a tool 'artifact' if it has EXACTLY ONE argument the server executes; if a tool takes more than one code-capable argument, keep it 'dave' (the gate validates a single field).
_verify_at_checkup: The server + tool names below are placeholders from the deprecated standalone MCP; the built-in server may differ. /checkup inventories the real ones — set 'server' to the real server name so tools from any OTHER MCP server are default-denied.
For each artifact/quarantine tool, confirm the exact argument NAME that carries the code / parent (the 'field' value) — the gate reads that field and nothing else.

Confirm the PreToolUse matcher 'mcp__.*' in settings.json fires on your Claude Code version (the gate canary proves it), and that /checkup added a per-tool allow for each auto/artifact tool so the child sees no prompt on the normal path.
